chapter_context.summary: Haman is promoted and demands bowing, but Mordecai refuses; enraged, Haman plots the complete extermination of all Jews, securing a royal decree and casting lots (Pur) to set the date.

outline.text: Haman Promoted; Mordecai Refuses to Bow Down to Him
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Haman Promoted; Mordecai Refuses to Bow Down to Him (3:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

outline.text: Haman's Anger; Plots Extermination and Obtains King's Consent
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Haman's Anger; Plots Extermination and Obtains King's Consent (3:6-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

outline.text: Decrees Written and Dispatched to All Provinces
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Decrees Written and Dispatched to All Provinces (3:12-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

key_themes.title: Unseen Providence
key_themes.description: Although God is not explicitly mentioned by name, His perfect timing, coordination of details, and deliverance are evident throughout the narrative.

key_themes.title: Courage and Duty
key_themes.description: Esther and Mordecai show that standing up for justice and protecting the vulnerable requires courage and taking personal risks.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 3, how can you recognize God's silent directing in your own life's circumstances?
What does it mean for you to act with courage 'for such a time as this' in your sphere of influence?

cultural_historical.title: The Persian Empire and Purim
cultural_historical.text: The events take place in Susa (Shushan), one of the capital cities of the Achaemenid Empire, and explain the origin of the Feast of Purim.
